photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: red brick rear elevation with buff brick arches and pale green painted doors, garage doors, gutters, and trim
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Rear elevations appear well maintained with uniform masonry, clean replacement windows, and little visible cracking or settlement.
photo_observations.window_details: Three adjoining houses show paired vertical windows on the main upper floors and narrow attic windows below the steep gables, all trimmed by buff-brick arches.
photo_observations.door_details: Each unit has a raised rear entry and a grade-level garage opening, both painted pale green.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative wood vergeboards and painted fascia trace the stepped rear gables.
photo_observations.porch_details: Concrete stoops with black railings rise from the lane to the rear doors.
photo_observations.chimney_details: One brick chimney is visible above the roofline near the middle of the grouped view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Buff-brick banding, patterned ventilation panels, and carved gable trim are the main decorative features.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Steep gabled roof with repeated rear-facing gable ends
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Replacement windows and overhead garage doors have been inserted into the rear ground floor.
photo_observations.notes: Closer rear-lane view centered on the eastern half of the Oxford Street row.

notes: Closer rear-lane view centered on the eastern half of the Oxford Street row.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a steep gabled roof with repeated rear-facing gable ends, consistent with its house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 6, 6, 3 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 3 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include string courses, cornice.

facade_description: 32E-32C Oxford St presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a steep gabled roof with repeated rear-facing gable ends, consistent with its house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 6, 6, 3 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 3 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include string courses, cornice.
